steps.title: Solve for n
steps.content:
Start with $$2n+6=14$$. Subtract 6 from both sides: $$2n=8$$. Divide both sides by 2: $$n=4$$.
This means the tree reaches 14 feet after 4 years of growth.

steps.title: Interpret the coefficient 2
steps.content:
The initial height is 6 feet. After 4 years the height is 14 feet, so total growth is $$14-6=8$$ feet.
Divide the total growth by the number of years: $$\frac{8}{4}=2$$ feet per year.
Thus the number 2 represents the average yearly growth in feet.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
